description.ko: 빠르게 절단되는 흑백 기하학의 파편들이 정밀한 균형 속에서 혼돈을 붙잡는 장면.
description.en: A high-speed monochrome field where fractured geometric blades and checksum fragments hold chaos in exact balance.

prompt: KURO-X artwork titled "Checksum Guillotine Matrix": a severe black and white geometric glitch abstraction with no character, logo, typography, sculpture, sphere, cube, monolith, or single central object. The frame is constructed as a suspended field of interlocking guillotine-like vector blades and checksum cells: matte #1A1A1A angular slabs shear against hard #FFFFFF data voids, while #808080 and #C0C0C0 clipped facets form staggered triangular counters, split hexagonal locks, thin diagonal cuts, and broken right-angle hinges. The composition feels like a corrupted verification system firing at high speed, with rows of fragments shifting in alternating directions yet resolving into exact visual equilibrium. Dense horizontal scanlines cross every layer; luma tearing, grayscale jitter, bit-crushed square noise, fractured parity marks, stepped edge echoes, and medium-density particle debris intensify the monochrome complexity. High contrast, crisp vector edges, sharp angles, cold mechanical depth, fast motion, chaos within order, perfect balance created by fragments.
